volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 395 - Section 3.620)

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: τίν ἀντιτάξεις τῷδε ; τίς Προίτου πυλῶν
sections.translation: Whom will you send against him ? Who will be capable of standing as our champion at the Proetid gate when its bars are loosened ?

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: κόσμον μὲν ἀνδρὸς οὔτιν ἂν τρέσαιμ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: I would not tremble before any mere ornaments on a man . Nor can signs and symbols wound and kill — crests and bell have no bite without the spear .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: καὶ νύκτα ταύτην ἣν λέγεις ἐπ ἀσπίδος
sections.translation: And regarding this " night " which you describe on his shield , sparkling with heaven ’ s stars — perhaps the folly of it might yield to one some prophetic understanding . For should night fall on this man ’ s eyes as he dies , then to its bearer this arrogant symbol

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: γένοιτ ἂν ὀρθῶς ἐνδίκως τ ἐπώνυμον ,
sections.translation: would prove rightly and justly named ; and it is against himself that he will have prophesied this outrageous violence . Now as for me , against Tydeus I will station the trusty son of Astacus as defender of this gate , since he is full noble and

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: τιμῶντα καὶ στυγοῦνθ ὑπέρφρονας λόγους .
sections.translation: reveres the throne of Honor and detests proud speech . He is slow to act disgracefully , and he has no cowardly nature . His race springs from the men sown of the dragon ’ s teeth , from one of those whom Ares spared , and so Melanippus is truly born of our land . Ares will decide the outcome with a throw of the dice ;

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: Δίκη δ ὁμαίμων [*] κάρτα νιν προστέλλεται
sections.translation: but Justice , his kin by blood , indeed sends this man forth to keep the enemy spear from the mother that gave him birth . Exit Melanippus .

sections.label: Section 1.417
sections.original: τὸν ἁμόν νυν ἀντίπαλον εὐτυχεῖν
sections.translation: May the gods grant success to our champion , since he rises up in a just cause , to battle for his city ! But I shudder

sections.label: Section 1.420
sections.original: φόρους μόρους ὑπὲρ φίλων
sections.translation: to watch the bloody deaths of men cut down for the sake of their own people .

sections.label: Section 1.422
sections.original: τούτῳ μὲν οὕτως εὐτυχεῖν δοῖεν θεοί : [*]
sections.translation: Yes , may the gods so grant success to this man . Capaneus is stationed at the Electran gates , another giant of a man , greater than the one described before .

sections.label: Section 1.425
sections.original: μείζων , ὁ κόμπος δ οὐ κατ ἄνθρωπον φρονεῖ ,
sections.translation: But his boast is too proud for a mere human , and he makes terrifying threats against our battlements — which , I hope , chance will not fulfil ! For he says he will utterly destroy the city with god ’ s will or without it , and that not even conflict with Zeus , though it should fall before him in the plain , will stand in his way .

sections.label: Section 1.430
sections.original: τὰς δ ἀστραπάς τε καὶ κεραυνίους βολὰς
sections.translation: The god ’ s lightning and thunderbolts he compares to midday heat . For his shield ’ s symbol he has a man without armor bearing fire , and the torch , his weapon , blazes in his hands ; and in golden letters he says I will burn the city .

sections.label: Section 1.435
sections.original: τοιῷδε φωτὶ πέμπε — τίς ξυστήσεται ,
sections.translation: Against such a man make your dispatch — who will meet him in combat , who will stand firm without trembling before his boasts ?

sections.label: Section 1.437
sections.original: καὶ τῷδε κόμπῳ [*] κέρδος ἄλλο τίκτεται .
sections.translation: Here too gain follows with interest from gain . [*] The tongue proves in the end to be an unerring accuser of men ’ s wicked thoughts .

sections.label: Section 1.440
sections.original: Καπανεὺς δ ἀπειλεῖ δρᾶν παρεσκευασμένος ,
sections.translation: Capaneus makes his threats , ready to act , irreverent toward the gods , and giving his tongue full exercise in wicked glee , he , though a mere mortal , sends a loud and swollen boast to Zeus in heaven . But I trust that the fire-bearing thunderbolt will justly come to him ,

sections.label: Section 1.445
sections.original: ἥξειν κεραυνόν , οὐδὲν ἐξῃκασμένον
sections.translation: and when it comes it will not be anything like the sun ’ s mid-day heat . And against him , even though he is a big talker , a man of fiery spirit , mighty Polyphontes , is stationed , a dependable sentinel

sections.label: Section 1.450
sections.original: Ἀρτέμιδος εὐνοίαισι σύν τ ἄλλοις θεοῖς .
sections.translation: with the good will of guardian Artemis and the other gods . Now tell me about another one allotted to other gates ! Exit Polyphontes .

sections.label: Section 1.452
sections.original: ὄλοιθ ὃς πόλει μεγάλ ἐπεύχεται ,
sections.translation: Death to him who exults so arrogantly over the city ! May the thunderbolt stop him before he leaps into my home

sections.label: Section 1.455
sections.original: ἑδωλίων μ [*] ὑπερκόπῳ [*]
sections.translation: and plunders me from my maiden chambers with his outrageous spear !

sections.label: Section 1.457
sections.original: [ καὶ μὴν τὸν ἐντεῦθεν λαχόντα πρὸς πύλαις ] [*]
sections.translation: Now I will tell you about the man who next drew station at the gates . The third lot leaped out of the upturned bronze helmet for Eteoclus ,

sections.label: Section 1.460
sections.original: πύλαισι Νηίσταισι προσβαλεῖν λόχον .
sections.translation: to hurl his band against the Neistan gates . He whirls his horses as they snort through their bridles , eager to fall against the gate . Their muzzles whistle in a barbarian way , filled with the breath of their haughty nostrils .

sections.label: Section 1.465
sections.original: ἐσχημάτισται [*] δ ἀσπὶς οὐ σμικρὸν [*] τρόπον :
sections.translation: His shield is decorated in great style : an armored man climbs a ladder ’ s rungs to mount an enemy tower that he wants to destroy . This one , too , shouts in syllables of written letters that even Ares could not hurl him from the battlements .

sections.label: Section 1.470
sections.original: καὶ τῷδε φωτὶ πέμπε τὸν φερέγγυον
sections.translation: Send a dependable opponent against this man , too , to keep the yoke of slavery from our city .

sections.label: Section 1.472
sections.original: πέμποιμ ἂν ἤδη τόνδε , σὺν τύχῃ δέ τῳ :
sections.translation: I would send this man here , and with good fortune . Exit Megareus . Indeed , he has already been sent , his only boast in his hands , Megareus , Creon ’ s seed , of the race of the sown- men .

sections.label: Section 1.475
sections.original: ὃς οὔτι μάργων ἱππικῶν φρυαγμάτων
sections.translation: He will not withdraw from the gate in fear of the thunder of the horses ’ furious snorting ; but either he will die and pay the earth the full price of his nurture , or will capture two men and the city on the shield , and then adorn his father ’ s house with the spoils .

sections.label: Section 1.480
sections.original: κόμπαζ ἐπ ἄλλῳ , μηδέ μοι φθόνει λόγων . [*]
sections.translation: Tell me about another ’ s boasts and do not begrudge me the full tale !

sections.label: Section 2.481
sections.original: ἐπεύχομαι τῷδε [*] μὲν εὐτυχεῖν , ἰὼ
sections.translation: O champion of my home , I pray that this man will have good fortune , and that there will be bad fortune for his enemies . As they boast too much against the city in their frenzied mind ,

sections.label: Section 2.485
sections.original: Ζεὺς νεμέτωρ ἐπίδοι κοταίνων .
sections.translation: so , too , may Zeus the Requiter look on them in anger !

sections.label: Section 2.486
sections.original: τέταρτος ἄλλος , γείτονας πύλας ἔχων
sections.translation: Another , the fourth , has the gate near Onca Athena and takes his stand with a shout , Hippomedon , tremendous in form and figure . I shuddered in fear as he spun a huge disk — the circle of his shield , I mean —

sections.label: Section 2.490
sections.original: ἔφριξα δινήσαντος : οὐκ ἄλλως ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: I cannot deny it . The symbol-maker who put the design on his shield was no lowly craftsman : the symbol is Typhon , spitting out of his fire-breathing mouth a dark , thick smoke , the darting sister of fire .

sections.label: Section 2.495
sections.original: ὄφεων δὲ πλεκτάναισι περίδρομον κύτος
sections.translation: And the rim of the hollow-bellied shield is fastened all around with snaky braids . The warrior himself has raised the war-cry and , inspired by Ares he raves for battle like a maenad , with a look to inspire fear . We must put up a good defense against the assault of such a man ,

sections.label: Section 2.500
sections.original: φόβος γὰρ ἤδη πρὸς πύλαις κομπάζεται .
sections.translation: for already Rout is boasting of victory at the gate .

sections.label: Section 2.501
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν Ὄγκα Παλλάς , ἥτ [*] ἀγχίπτολις ,
sections.translation: First Onca Pallas , who dwells near the city , close by the gate , and who loathes outrageousness in a man , will fend him off like a dangerous snake away from nestlings . Moreover , Hyperbius , Oenops ’ trusty son ,

sections.label: Section 2.505
sections.original: ἀνὴρ κατ ἄνδρα τοῦτον ᾑρέθη , θέλων
sections.translation: is chosen to match him , man to man , as he is eager to search out his fate in the crisis that chance has wrought — neither in form , nor spirit nor in the wielding of his arms does he bear reproach . Hermes [*] has appropriately pitted them against each other . For the man is hostile to the man he faces in battle ,

sections.label: Section 2.510
sections.original: ξυνοίσετον δὲ πολεμίους ἐπ ἀσπίδων
sections.translation: and the gods on their shields also meet as enemies . The one has fire-breathing Typhon , while father Zeus stands upright on Hyperbius ’ shield , his lightening bolt aflame in his hand . And no one yet has seen Zeus conquered .

sections.label: Section 2.515
sections.original: τοιάδε μέντοι προσφίλεια δαιμόνων .
sections.translation: Such then is the favor of the divine powers : we are with the victors , they with the vanquished , if Zeus in fact proves stronger in battle than Typhon . And it is likely that the mortal adversaries will fare as do their gods ; and so , in accordance with the symbol ,

sections.label: Section 2.520
sections.original: σωτὴρ γένοιτ ἂν Ζεὺς ἐπ ἀσπίδος τυχών .
sections.translation: Zeus will be a savior for Hyperbius since he resides on his shield . Exit Hyperbius .

sections.label: Section 2.521
sections.original: πέποιθα δὴ [*] τὸν Διὸς ἀντίτυπον ἔχοντ
sections.translation: I am sure that Zeus ’ antagonist , since he has on his shield the unloved form of an earth-born deity , an image hated by both mortals and the long-lived gods ,

sections.label: Section 2.525
sections.original: πρόσθε πυλᾶν κεφαλὰν ἰάψειν .
sections.translation: will drop his head in death before the gate .

sections.label: Section 2.526
sections.original: οὕτως γένοιτο . τὸν δὲ πέμπτον αὖ λέγω ,
sections.translation: Let it be so ! Next I describe the fifth man who is stationed at the fifth , the Northern gate opposite the tomb of Amphion , Zeus ’ s son . He swears by his spear which , in his confidence , he holds more to be revered than a god

sections.label: Section 2.530
sections.original: σέβειν πεποιθὼς ὀμμάτων θ ὑπέρτερον ,
sections.translation: and more precious than his eyes , that he will sack the city of the Cadmeans in spite of Zeus . He says this , the beautiful child of a mountain-bred mother — a warrior , half man , half boy , and his beard ’ s first growth is just now advancing on his cheeks ,

sections.label: Section 2.535
sections.original: ὥρας φυούσης , ταρφὺς ἀντέλλουσα θρίξ .
sections.translation: his youth in first bloom , thick , upspringing hair . But now he makes his advance with a savage heart and a terrifying look , not at all like the maidens he ’ s named for . [*] Nor does he take his stand at the gate unboasting , but wields our city ’ s shame on his bronze-forged

sections.label: Section 2.540
sections.original: σάκει , κυκλωτῷ σώματος προβλήματι ,
sections.translation: shield , his body ’ s circular defence , on which the Sphinx who eats men raw is cleverly fastened with bolts , her body embossed and gleaming . She carries under her a single Cadmean , so that against this man chiefly our missiles will be hurled .

sections.label: Section 2.545
sections.original: ἐλθὼν δ ἔοικεν οὐ καπηλεύσειν μάχην ,
sections.translation: He does not seem to have come to do any petty trading in the battle , nor to shame the making of his long journey — he is Parthenopaeus of Arcadia . Such is the man , and aiming to make full payment for the fine support given him in Argos , his adopted land , he now threatens our fortifications — may God not fulfil his threats !

sections.label: Section 2.550
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ τύχοιεν ὧν φρονοῦσι πρὸς θεῶν ,
sections.translation: If only they would get from the gods what they wish for , because of those unholy boasts of theirs , then surely they would perish in utter ruin and misery . There is a man for this one , too , whom you name an Arcadian , a man who does not boast , but who knows the thing to do —

sections.label: Section 2.555
sections.original: Ἄκτωρ ἀδελφὸς τοῦ πάρος λελεγμένου :
sections.translation: Actor , brother of him I named before . He will not allow words that lack deeds to overrun his gate and increase fear , nor will he let in a man who carries on his hostile shield the image of the ravenous , detested beast .

sections.label: Section 2.560
sections.original: ἣ ʼξωθεν [*] εἴσω τῷ φέροντι μέμψεται ,
sections.translation: That beast outside his shield will blame the man who carries her into the gate , when she has taken a heavy beating beneath the city ’ s walls . If the gods are willing , what I speak may prove true ! Exit Actor .

sections.label: Section 3.563
sections.original: ἱκνεῖται λόγος διὰ στηθέων ,
sections.translation: His words penetrate to my heart , my hair stands on end

sections.label: Section 3.565
sections.original: μεγάλα μεγαληγόρων κλυούσᾳ [*]
sections.translation: as I hear the loud threats of these loud-boasting , impious men . May the gods destroy them here in our land !

sections.label: Section 3.568
sections.original: ἕκτον λέγοιμ ἂν ἄνδρα σωφρονέστατον ,
sections.translation: The sixth man I will name is of the highest moderation and a seer brave in combat , mighty Amphiaraus .

sections.label: Section 3.570
sections.original: Ὁμολωίσιν δὲ πρὸς πύλαις τεταγμένος
sections.translation: Stationed at the Homoloid gate , he repeatedly rebukes mighty Tydeus with evil names Murderer , maker of unrest in the city , principal teacher of evils to the Argives , summoner of vengeance ’ s Curse , servant of Slaughter , counselor to Adrastus in these evil plans . And next , with eyes looking upward , he addressed your own brother , mighty Polynices who shares your blood , and called him by name , dwelling twice upon its latter part . [*] These were his words :

sections.label: Section 3.580
sections.original: “ ἦ τοῖον ἔργον καὶ θεοῖσι προσφιλές ,
sections.translation: Will such a deed as this be pleasing to the gods , fine to hear of and to relate to those in the future — that you sacked the city of your ancestors and your native gods and launched a foreign army against them ? What justice is it to drain dry the font of your existence ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.585
sections.original: πατρὶς δὲ γαῖα σῆς ὑπὸ σπουδῆς δορὶ
sections.translation: And how shall your fatherland , captured by the spear for the sake of your ambition , be won over to your cause ? As for me , I will enrich this earth , a seer interred beneath enemy soil . Let us fight ! I anticipate no dishonorable death .

sections.label: Section 3.590
sections.original: τοιαῦθ ὁ μάντις ἀσπίδ εὔκυκλον νέμων [*]
sections.translation: So the seer spoke as untroubled he held his all-bronze shield . No symbol was fixed to his shield ’ s circle . For he does not wish to appear the bravest , but to be the bravest , as he harvests the fruit of his mind ’ s deep furrow , where his careful resolutions grow .

sections.label: Section 3.595
sections.original: τούτῳ σοφούς τε κἀγαθοὺς ἀντηρέτας
sections.translation: I advise you to send wise and brave opponents against him . He who reveres the gods is to be feared .

sections.label: Section 3.597
sections.original: φεῦ τοῦ ξυναλλάσσοντος ὄρνιθος βροτοῖς [*]
sections.translation: Ah , the pity of fate ’ s omen when it makes a just man associate with the irreverent ! In all things , nothing is more evil

sections.label: Section 3.600
sections.original: κάκιον οὐδέν , καρπὸς οὐ κομιστέος :
sections.translation: than evil partnership . Its fruit should not be gathered in : the field of recklessness yields a harvest of death . For it may be that a pious man , embarked shipboard with sailors hot for some crime , perishes along with the sort of men hated by the gods ;

sections.label: Section 3.605
sections.original: ἢ ξὺν πολίταις ἀνδράσιν δίκαιος ὢν
sections.translation:
or , a man , though upright himself , when among fellow-citizens who hate all strangers and neglect the gods , may fall undeserving into the same trap as they , and be subdued , struck by the scourge of God that strikes all alike .
Just so the seer , Oecles ’ son ,

sections.label: Section 3.610
sections.original: σώφρων δίκαιος ἀγαθὸς εὐσεβὴς ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: although a moderate , just , noble , reverent man and a great prophet , mixes with impious , rash-talking men against his own judgment , men stretching out in a procession that is long to retrace , [*] and , if it is Zeus ’ s will , he will be be dragged down in ruin along with them .

sections.label: Section 3.615
sections.original: δοκῶ μὲν οὖν σφε μηδὲ προσβαλεῖν πύλαις ,
sections.translation: So then , I expect that he will not even charge the gates : not because he lacks courage or is weak-willed , but because he knows that he must meet his end in battle , if the prophecies of Loxias are to come to fruition — the god usually either holds silent or speaks to the point .

sections.label: Section 3.620
sections.original: ὅμως δ ἐπ αὐτῷ φῶτα , Λασθένους βίαν ,
sections.translation: Just the same , I will station a man against him , mighty Lasthenes , a gate-keeper who hates foreigners . He has the wisdom of an old man , but his body is at its prime : his eyes are quick , and he does not let his hand delay for his spear to seize what is left exposed by the shield .
